title: Unreal Interface Tour
learningTarget: I can identify the main Unreal interface panels and explain how each one supports building and organizing a 3D world.

bellRinger.prompt: Which software interface have you used that felt complicated at first, and what helped you learn it?

vocabulary.definition: The main window that shows the 3D world and lets users select or manipulate objects.
The row of tools used to add objects, switch modes, play, and access common actions.
A list of objects in the current level, useful for selecting, organizing, hiding, and grouping.
The panel that shows editable properties for the selected object.
The asset browser that can open from the bottom of the editor, often with Ctrl+Space.
The docked version of the Content Drawer that shows project folders and assets.

assignment.title: Unreal Interface Map
evidenceRequired: Annotated screenshot or form response identifying viewport, toolbar, outliner, details panel, and content drawer

exitTicket: Which panel shows the selected object's editable settings?
